channel.title: Vitals & Signals
channel.description: I started Vitals & Signals because I needed a better way to stay current at the intersection of AI and healthcare — so I built one. Every week, I use AI to research, write, and produce this show: scanning the news, following the money, and flagging what actually matters. What started as a personal tool became something worth sharing. If you're a founder, investor, clinician, or just AI-curious, this is your weekly briefing — built by a guy who wanted one and couldn't find it.
channel.itunes:author: Vitals & Signals

item.title: Ep. 004 — The Same AI That Heals You Can Hack You
item.description: Physicians are already building their own clinical tools — responsibly, with patient privacy designed in from day one. The question isn't whether they should. It's whether the EHRs, the hospitals, and a privacy law written in 1996 for a fax-first world will open up to meet them — or keep forcing innovation to happen in the shadows.

item.title: Ep. 003 — The Ambient AI Scribe Billing Bombshell Payers Have Been Waiting For
item.description: The ambient AI scribe industry sold itself on burnout relief — but the dirty secret hiding in plain sight is that better documentation means higher billing codes, and a peer-reviewed JAMA paper plus a $2.3B BCBSA study just handed payers the ammunition to declare war.

item.title: Ep. 002 — The FDA Is Moving the Goalposts on Clinical AI
item.description: The FDA is quietly rewriting the fundamental contract of clinical AI regulation — moving from a pre-market permission slip to a post-market surveillance regime — and every founder, investor, and clinician in this industry needs to understand what that means before the framework lands.

item.title: Ep. 001 — Doctronic and the OxyContin Exploit
item.description: Utah handed an AI the authority to prescribe medications, a security firm broke it in a single conversation, and the FDA has never cleared a single LLM-based medical device — this week, the gap between how fast healthcare is deploying AI and how slowly the regulatory system is catching up became impossible to ignore.
